g.text: Bits 29-16 | 14 bits | size | sizeof(arg_type), max 16383 bytes — used by strace
Bits 15-08 | 8 bits | magic/type | ASCII char identifying driver e.g. 0x6B = 'k'
Bits 07-00 | 8 bits | nr (seq) | Sequential command number within this driver: 0-255
macro: _IOC(dir, type, nr, size) — all four macros expand to this
DIR | SIZE (14 bits) | TYPE (8 bits) | NR (8 bits)
DIR encoding: 00=_IO 01=_IOW(write to kern) 10=_IOR(read from kern) 11=_IOWR
bits 31-30 | 01 | write — userspace sends ulong to kernel
ioctl(fd, MYDEVICE_IOC_RESIZE, and_new_size) // arg is ptr to ulong
ioctl(fd, MYDEVICE_IOC_CLEAR) // third arg ignored by kernel
bits 31-30 | 10 | read — kernel sends struct to userspace
ioctl(fd, MYDEVICE_IOC_STATUS, and_status) // arg is ptr to struct
WARNING: _IOC_WRITE means kernel writes to user (_IOR from user view)
WARNING: _IOC_WRITE means user writes to kernel (_IOW from user view)
Key: naming is from USERSPACE perspective; _IOC bits are KERNEL perspective
_IOC_SIZE(cmd) | extract bits 29-16 | used by access_ok() for range check
_IOC_DIR(cmd) | extract bits 31-30 | check if access_ok needed
Step 3 | dir and _IOC_READ: access_ok | return -EFAULT (bad user ptr)
Step 4 | dir and _IOC_WRITE: access_ok | return -EFAULT (bad user ptr)
Step 5 | switch(cmd) dispatch | call resize / clear / status
ENOTTY = 'Inappropriate ioctl for device' — correct errno for unknown cmd
strace reads bits 31-30 -> direction=read -> decodes output struct
strace reads bits 29-16 -> size=40 -> reads 40 bytes from user ptr
strace reads bits 15-8 -> type=0x6b='k' -> driver identifier
strace reads bits 7-0 -> nr=2 -> sequence number
Without size in cmd bits: strace could not decode arg without debug info
This is WHY size is encoded in command — enables zero-recompile tracing
struct mydevice_status | unsigned long buffer_size | 0x00 | 8 bytes
struct mydevice_status | unsigned long bytes_used | 0x08 | 8 bytes
struct mydevice_status | unsigned int open_count | 0x10 | 4 bytes
struct mydevice_status | unsigned long read_count | 0x18 | 8 bytes
struct mydevice_status | unsigned long write_count | 0x20 | 8 bytes
Each driver should use a UNIQUE 8-bit magic char
Collision risk: wrong driver receives your command number
Without magic check: _IOC_TYPE validation catches misdirected ioctls
With magic check: ENOTTY returned for cross-driver accidents
Your driver 'k'=0x6B — verify unregistered before shipping
